steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find the slope of the tangent line
steps.content: A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius from center $(-2,2)$ to $(6,-4)$ has slope $\dfrac{-4-2}{6-(-2)}=\dfrac{-6}{8}=-\dfrac{3}{4}$, so the tangent slope is the negative reciprocal: $\dfrac{4}{3}$.

steps.title: Write the equation of line t
steps.content: Using point-slope form through $(6,-4)$: $y-(-4)=\dfrac{4}{3}(x-6)$, which simplifies to $y=\dfrac{4}{3}x-8-4=\dfrac{4}{3}x-12$.

steps.title: Test each point in the line equation
